meta.title: Strategy in the Age of Generative AI

slides.headline: Strategy in the Age of Generative AI
slides.subheadline: Professor Andy Wu · Harvard Business School

slides.headline: The Fastest Adoption Curve in History
slides.subheadline: Chatbots reached 25% US penetration faster than every prior technology — smartphones, internet, TV, radio.

slides.headline: Every Decade Reshuffles the Board
slides.subheadline: Will AI be the next paradigm shift?
bullets: 1970s: Mini-Mainframe — IBM dominates enterprise computing
1980s: Personal Computer — Microsoft & Apple emerge
1990s: Internet — Google, Amazon, Yahoo rise
2000s: Mobile + Social — Apple, Facebook, Twitter win
2020s: AI? — profit redistribution underway
slides.note: Each shift creates new market leaders and destroys incumbents.

slides.headline: Is Gen AI a Revolution — or Evolution?

left.title: Myth
bullets: A surprise disruption no one saw coming
Fundamentally discontinuous from prior tech
Impossible to anticipate or plan around

right.title: Reality
bullets: A natural evolution in computing power
Driven by Huang's Law: GPU cost falls ~2× per year
Global data grew from 2 ZB (2010) to 181 ZB (2025)
Managers can and must anticipate the next 5+ years

slides.headline: The AI Stack: What Is Generative AI?
slides.subheadline: Transformer models sit at the core of every modern GenAI product.
bullets: Artificial Intelligence: machines that simulate cognition
Machine Learning: systems that improve from data
Deep Learning: multi-layer neural networks
Transformer: attention-based architecture powering LLMs

slides.headline: Gen AI Augments Computing in Two Ways
bullets: INPUTS — Abstraction: natural language replaces code, clicks, and forms; anyone can now operate specialized software
OUTPUTS — Computation: AI expands what software can produce — text, images, code, decisions, predictions
slides.note: More people will use more computers for specialized purposes — but not necessarily the same people.

slides.headline: Where Gen AI Actually Creates Value

left.title: The Conversation
bullets: Focus: improving quality of outputs
Gen AI raises 'Worth to Customer'
Drives willingness-to-pay higher

right.title: The Strategic Insight
bullets: Focus: dramatically cutting cost structure
Gen AI lowers 'Cost to Supplier'
Ryanair beat BA not on quality — on cost
AI-enabled low-cost entrants will do the same

slides.headline: The AI Adoption Matrix
slides.subheadline: Right approach depends on data type and cost of error.
bullets: No Regrets (Explicit data, Low error cost): AI Agent — fully autonomous, no human in loop
Quality Control (Explicit data, High error cost): Specialist + AI — AI produces, human verifies
Creative Catalyst (Tacit knowledge, Low error cost): Generalist + AI — AI options, human selects
Human First (Tacit knowledge, High error cost): Expert leads — AI assists at the margins
slides.note: Adopt early even when capabilities are imperfect if cost savings are substantial and error costs are low.

slides.headline: Gen AI Threatens Industry Profitability
slides.subheadline: Wide availability is a weakness, not a strength.
bullets: Generic adoption reduces barriers to entry — new competitors flood every market
Supplier bargaining power shifts: in-house legal roles grew 230%+ since 1997 as AI tools empower buyers
Human capital at scale becomes variable cost — fixed-cost moats erode
Technology reshuffles markets: Walmart → Amazon, Print NYT → Digital NYT
Blockbuster saw Netflix coming in 2000 — and still went bankrupt in 2010
slides.note: Competing in the AI age requires counterintuitive decisions: disrupt yourself before rivals do.

slides.headline: Three Implementation Modes
slides.subheadline: Choose based on customization needs and usage scope.
bullets: Customize Model: fine-tune on proprietary data (e.g., BloombergGPT) — company-specific, highest control
Integrate APIs: pipe foundation models into your product (e.g., Coca-Cola × OpenAI) — balanced flexibility
Purchase Applications: buy vertical AI tools (e.g., Harvey for legal) — fastest time-to-value

slides.headline: Strategic Transformation Playbook
bullets: Rethink all assets as data — physical spaces, conversations, workflows are training signals
Redesign the entire organization — don't layer AI on broken processes
Create more value with AI than competitors or margins will compress
Act now: waiting for 'readiness' cedes first-mover advantage

slides.headline: Five Takeaways
slides.subheadline: Professor Andy Wu · Harvard Business School
bullets: Gen AI will keep improving — anticipating the trajectory is your job
It fundamentally changes who can access and operate software
Adopt even when imperfect if cost savings justify it
Competition will intensify — prepare to self-disrupt and take calculated risks
Two essential questions: Where is your data? What must your org become?

slides.headline: The Question That Matters
slides.subheadline: "By three methods we may learn wisdom: by reflection, which is noblest; by imitation, which is easiest; and by experience, which is the bitterest." — Confucius
